verifier_input.doc_snippet:
On-time graduation is widely recognised as a key performance indicator in higher education, reflecting both institutional effectiveness and student academic success. With the increasing adoption of learning management systems (LMS) and student information systems (SIS), universities now collect large volumes of academic data that can be leveraged to analyse student progression and performance patterns [1, 2]. The emergence of learning analytics has further enabled institutions to extract actionable insights to improve student outcomes and institutional decision-making [3].
